citation: De Arruda et al. (2025) - Cost-benefit analysis of solar energy integration in buildings
description: Section-skeleton few-shot example for building entity extraction. Blue-highlighted passages from the annotated PDF are preserved as relevant_snippets.
section_skeleton:
Paper structure (De Arruda et al., 2025 — 14 pages, ~65,000 characters):
--- TITLE & ABSTRACT (page 1) ---
[...~2000 chars about cost-benefit analysis of solar energy integration in affordable housing in Brazil...]
--- INTRODUCTION (pages 1-3) ---
[...~8000 chars of background on solar energy adoption, literature review, policy context — IGNORE THIS]
--- METHODOLOGY (pages 3-5) ---
[...~10000 chars describing general cost-benefit analysis methodology, software tools, simulation parameters — IGNORE THIS]
--- CASE STUDY (pages 4-6) --- ← LOOK HERE
"To validate the proposed methodology, a case study is presented. The chosen subject is Brazilian affordable housing, considering a single house from the Brazilian federal government program called 'Minha Casa Minha Vida,' Brazil's first-ever effort at large-scale public housing."
"For the case study, the Vale da Mata condominium was selected."
"The condominium comprises a total of 215 residential housing units, all of which are endowed with fundamental infrastructure, encompassing an expansive area measuring 9,838.99 square meters. It is situated within the district of the municipality of Duque de Caxias, in the city of Rio de Janeiro."
--- RESULTS (pages 7-12) ---
[...~25000 chars of simulation results for Models 1, 2, 3, financial analysis, CO2 reduction data — IGNORE THIS]
--- CONCLUSION (pages 12-14) ---
[...~5000 chars summarizing findings and recommendations — IGNORE THIS]

relevant_snippets.text: To validate the proposed methodology, a case study is presented. The chosen subject is Brazilian affordable housing, considering a single house from the Brazilian federal government program called 'Minha Casa Minha Vida,' Brazil's first-ever effort at large-scale public housing.
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Identifies the case study building type and program context

relevant_snippets.text: For the case study, the Vale da Mata condominium was selected.
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Names the specific case study building

relevant_snippets.text: The condominium comprises a total of 215 residential housing units, all of which are endowed with fundamental infrastructure, encompassing an expansive area measuring 9,838.99 square meters. It is situated within the district of the municipality of Duque de Caxias, in the city of Rio de Janeiro.
relevant_snippets.why_relevant: Provides building size, unit count, and precise location (city, district)

name.context: For the case study, the Vale da Mata condominium was selected

country.context: Brazilian affordable housing, considering a single house from the Brazilian federal government program

city.context: situated within the district of the municipality of Duque de Caxias, in the city of Rio de Janeiro

lesson: From this entire 14-page paper (~65,000 characters), only 3 short snippets from pages 4-6 contained building entity information. The model should focus on 'Case Study' and 'Methods' sections and IGNORE introduction, literature review, results, and conclusion sections.
